rulings.Case Title: SFBA INVESTMENT GROUP, LLC VS. JAYDIPSINH DEVDHARA ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Hearing On Devdharas' Petition To Confirm Arbitration Award
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for June 10, 2021 line 1. RESPONDENTS JAYDIPSINH DEVDHARA and VIKRAMSINH DEVDHARA's Notice Of Hearing On Petition To Confirm Arbitration Award is continued to June 14, 2021 to be heard with other pending motions. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303; or Phone Dial in: (415) 762-9988; Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: UMPQUA BANK VS. XELAN PROP 1, LLC. ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion For Order Enforcing August 14, 2020 Order Compelling Discovery Responses From Umpqua Bank Prior To Entry Of Judgment
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for June 10, 2021 line 2. DEFENDANT XELAN PROP 1, LLC's Notice Of Motion For Order Enforcing August 14, 2020 Order Compelling Discovery Responses From Umpqua Bank Prior To Entry Of Judgment is DENIED without prejudice for Defendant to renew the motion if necessary and proper at such time that their defenses and claims as outlined in paragraph 1 of August 14, 2020 again become an issue in this case. As to the issues refenced at page 5: 20-24 of the Moving papers, Defendants did not identify and specific discovery that would be responsive to the potential outstanding matters in this action. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303; or Phone Dial in: (415) 762-9988; Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: TONY GARNICKI ET AL VS. ANCHOR LOANS LP, ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION TO CONSOLIDATE ACTIONS OF CASE(S) CGC-20-584439, CGC-20-584440, CUD-20-667549, CUD-20-667550, CUD-20-667551 WITH CASE CGC-20-584236
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for June 10, 2021 line 6. PLAINTIFFS TONY GARNICKI, OCEAN AVENUE REAL ESTATE FUND LLC, and SANCHEZ RE, LLC'S MOTION TO CONSOLIDATE ACTIONS OF CASE(S) CGC-20-584439, CGC-20-584440, CUD-20-667549, CUD-20-667550, CUD-20-667551 WITH CASE CGC-20-584236 is GRANTED. The actions are consolidated for discovery and trial. Untimely served declarations by JRM Financial are not considered. Had the declarations been properly filed and served by May 21, 2021, the objections would have been sustained for lack of foundation as to how the declarant knows the information in the declaration. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303; or Phone Dial in: (415) 762-9988; Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.
